title: Recognizing the benefits of natural gas to the United States economy and environment, and recognizing natural gas as an affordable and "green" energy.
plain_summary: This House resolution says that natural gas is a clean and cheap energy source that helps the United States. It claims natural gas has made the air cleaner, helped American friends in other countries, and made America safer. The resolution supports making more natural gas in America and getting rid of rules that make it harder to produce. It also wants to make it easier to build places that can send natural gas to other countries.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Natural Resources, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of su

sections.title: Title and Introduction
sections.text: This is House Resolution fifty-seven from the one hundred nineteenth Congress. It was introduced on January twenty-third, two thousand twenty-five by Representative Balderson and three other representatives. The resolution recognizes the benefits of natural gas to the United States economy and environment, and calls natural gas an affordable and green energy source. The resolution was sent to two committees for review: the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Supporting Facts and Background
sections.text: The resolution lists many facts to support its position on natural gas. It states that the Inflation Reduction Act of two thousand twenty-two created a penalty fee on methane emissions (a gas that comes from natural gas production). This fee will act like a tax that hurts all Americans, especially those with low incomes. Any tax or fee on natural gas will make it more expensive for families, farmers, and small businesses to use natural gas. These costs will especially hurt areas where natural gas is produced and the businesses involved in making and selling natural gas. Since two thousand five, two-thirds of the reduction in greenhouse gas emissions from power plants happened because they switched to using natural gas instead of other fuels. Even though the United States nearly doubled its natural gas production between two thousand five and two thousand twenty, methane emissions dropped by ten percent during that same time because of better technology. The Energy Information Administration says that using natural gas to make electricity creates fewer harmful air pollutants, including carbon dioxide, compared to other fossil fuels like coal and oil. The Environmental Protection Agency reports that since nineteen seventy, combined emissions of major air pollutants in the United States have dropped by seventy-eight percent. In two thousand twenty-one, forty-five percent of the European Union's natural gas came from Russia, which was one hundred fifty-five billion cubic meters of liquefied natural gas (natural gas that has been cooled until it becomes a liquid for easier shipping). On March ninth, two thousand twenty-two, the European Commission approved rules that officially consider certain natural gas and nuclear activities as green investments. This decision allows natural gas and nuclear energy to be called renewable or green investments, which will encourage more private and public money to be invested in these energy sources and help Europe depend less on Russian energy. On March twenty-fifth, two thousand twenty-two, the United States made an agreement with the European Union to supply up to fifteen billion additional cubic meters of liquefied natural gas by the end of two thousand twenty-two. In November two thousand twenty-two, another energy security agreement suggested that Europe would try to import up to one hundred forty-seven billion cubic meters of liquefied natural gas in two thousand twenty-three. On January twenty-sixth, two thousand twenty-four, President Biden announced a ban on approving new liquefied natural gas export permits to countries that do not have free trade agreements with the United States. United States liquefied natural gas exports helped reduce natural gas prices in Europe by more than eighty-three percent from two thousand twenty-two levels. The Department of Energy's National Energy Technology Laboratory estimates that American liquefied natural gas exported to Europe is forty-one percent cleaner than Russian liquefied natural gas exported to Europe. The International Energy Agency predicts that global demand for natural gas will increase by fifty-seven percent by two thousand fifty, and an additional two hundred forty billion cubic meters per year of liquefied natural gas export capacity will be needed by two thousand fifty. Using more natural gas in the United States will also reduce the country's dependence on foreign energy and make national security stronger. Natural gas is abundant in the United States, and the Energy Information Administration estimates the United States has enough dry natural gas to last nearly a century, with the potential for even more as technology and exploration improve.

sections.title: What the House Resolves
sections.text: Therefore, the House of Representatives resolves three things. First, it recognizes natural gas produced in the United States as an affordable and green energy source. Second, it recognizes that the United States should be committed to using all types of energy sources to meet the country's energy needs, and that natural gas is necessary for the United States to be energy dominant. Third, it supports efforts by the Trump administration, including the Department of Energy and the Department of the Interior, to increase domestic production of natural gas and natural gas infrastructure, identify and remove barriers to producing natural gas, and speed up the approval of new liquefied natural gas export facilities in the United States.
